subject: Continuous Random Variables
question: The amount of time $X$ (in minutes) it takes a person standing in line at a post office to reach the counter is described by the continuous probability function: $f(x) = \begin{cases} kxe^{-x} & x > 0 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ where $k$ is a constant. (a) Determine the value of $k$. (b) What is the probability that a person takes more than 1 minute to reach the counter?
